_README: This is the TEMPLATE config. Edit it for your own deployment, or copy it to examples/<your-instance>/config.json and point APP_CONFIG_PATH at that copy (see DEPLOY.md). A complete, filled-in example lives in examples/northwind-health/config.json.

branding.name: Contract Intelligence
branding.tagline: Your data and your documents, answered together

home.title: Contract Intelligence
home.description: Ask questions about your data and your documents. The assistant queries your tables through a Genie space and searches your documents through grounded retrieval, then answers with the numbers and the source text together.
home._suggestions_help: Optional. Starter prompt buttons shown on the empty home screen. Tailor them to your domain.
suggestions: Summarize what this assistant can help me with.
What questions can I ask about my data?
What can you find in my documents?
Give me an example of a question that uses both my data and my documents.

agent._comment: The agent's persona and tools. 'backend' must be 'genie' (a Genie space) or 'vector_search' (the rag index below). Add/rename tools freely; the descriptions are what the model routes on.
agent._multi_genie_help: You can list SEVERAL genie tools, each with its own "space_id" + a description of what that space covers, so the agent routes a question to the right space. A genie tool with no "space_id" uses the default genie.space_id below.
agent.system_prompt: You are a data assistant. Answer questions using the tools provided. Some tools query structured data (returning tables); others search documents (returning grounded text with citations). For a question that needs both, call the relevant tools and combine the results into one clear answer. Quote the citations returned by document tools and state numbers precisely. If a tool is not configured or returns an error, say so plainly and answer with whatever you do have.

tools.name: query_data
tools.description: Answer a quantitative/analytical question by running governed SQL over the data via the Genie space. Returns a table and the SQL.
tools.question_description: A clear, self-contained natural-language data question.
tools._space_id_help: Optional. Set "space_id": "<id>" to pin this tool to a specific Genie space; omit it to use the default genie.space_id.

tools.name: search_documents
tools.description: Answer a question from the document corpus using grounded retrieval. Returns an answer with source citations.
tools.question_description: A clear, self-contained natural-language question about the documents.
tools._scope_filter_help: Optional. To keep a question about one entity from being answered with another's similarly-worded text, add: "scope_filter": {"column": "source_file", "entities": {"<Name>": {"aliases": ["..."], "values": ["file1.pdf"]}}}

_auth_mode_help: How the app authenticates user-scoped calls (Genie). 'obo' (default) = run as the signed-in user so Unity Catalog permissions/masks apply, with a service-principal fallback; 'service_principal' = always use the app's service principal. Override with the AUTH_MODE env var in app.yaml.

supervisor._comment: The model that runs the agent's tool-calling loop. Any chat (OpenAI-style) serving endpoint in your workspace.

rag._comment: The document-search (RAG) tool. Point vector_search_index at the index built by notebooks/02_build_document_search_index.py. generation_endpoint writes the grounded answer.

genie._comment: The DEFAULT Genie space (used by any genie tool that does not set its own space_id). Create it in the Databricks UI on your tables, curate its instructions, then paste its space id here (or set GENIE_SPACE_ID). See notebooks/03_create_genie_space.py and DEPLOY.md.

history._comment: delta (default, uses the SQL warehouse + a Delta table) or lakebase (managed Postgres). Override with HISTORY_BACKEND.
